perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party may make disclosure s required by law or court order provided the Receiving Party uses diligent reasonable efforts to limit disclosure and has allowed the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party may make disclosure s required by law or court order; however, the Receiving Party must obtain written consent from the Disclosing Party prior to making any such disclosure, in addition to using diligent reasonable efforts to limit disclosure and allowing the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a conflicting requirement for disclosure. The original text allows disclosures required by law or court order if reasonable efforts are made to limit disclosure and the Disclosing Party is allowed to seek a protective order. The added clause now requires *prior written consent* from the Disclosing Party *in addition* to the existing requirements. However, court orders and legal requirements are time sensitive and can't wait for consent to be granted, making this contradiction impossible to enforce. This creates a contradiction because legally mandated disclosures may not be possible if the Disclosing Party refuses consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party may make disclosure s required by law or court order; however, the Receiving Party must obtain written consent from the Disclosing Party prior to making any such disclosure, in addition to using diligent reasonable efforts to limit disclosure and allowing the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order. 3. Immediately upon the written request by the Disclosing Party at any time, th e Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party all Proprietary Information and all documents or media containing any such Proprietary Information and any and all copies or extr acts thereof, save that where such Proprietary Information is a form inca pable of return or has been copied or transcribed into another document, it shall be destroyed or erased, as appropriate.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a requirement for prior written consent from the Disclosing Party before the Receiving Party can make disclosures required by law or court order. This directly contradicts the original allowance for disclosures required by law or court order, as legal mandates often cannot wait for consent, making the added requirement impossible to enforce in those situations. Since the Changed Text and Contradicted Text are the same, that doesn't negate the fact that it is a contradiction. The original text allows disclosures under specific circumstances and the changed text makes it so that those disclosures can't be made even when those circumstances are met.

perturbation.original_text: Immediately upon the written request by the Disclosing Party at any time, th e Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party all Proprietary Information and all documents or media containing any such Proprietary Information and any and all copies or extr acts thereof, save that where such Proprietary Information is a form inca pable of return or has been copied or transcribed into another document, it shall be destroyed or erased, as appropriate.
perturbation.changed_text: Immediately upon the written request by the Disclosing Party at any time, th e Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party all Proprietary Information and all documents or media containing any such Proprietary Information and any and all copies or extr acts thereof, save that where such Proprietary Information is a form inca pable of return or has been copied or transcribed into another document, it shall be destroyed or erased, as appropriate. However, the Receiving Party may keep one copy of the Proprietary Information for archival purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates a direct conflict. The original clause mandates the return or destruction of all Proprietary Information upon request. The added sentence permits the Receiving Party to keep one copy for archival purposes. This directly contradicts the requirement to return *all* information, leading to uncertainty about compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Immediately upon the written request by the Disclosing Party at any time, th e Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party all Proprietary Information and all documents or media containing any such Proprietary Information and any and all copies or extr acts thereof, save that where such Proprietary Information is a form inca pable of return or has been copied or transcribed into another document, it shall be destroyed or erased, as appropriate. However, the Receiving Party may keep one copy of the Proprietary Information for archival purposes. 4. The Receiving Party understands that nothing herein (i) requires the disclosure of any Proprietary Information or (ii) requir es the Disclosing Party to proceed with any transaction or relationship.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception allowing the Receiving Party to keep one copy of Proprietary Information for archival purposes, directly contradicting the original requirement to return *all* such information. The original text and contradicted text are in the same location. The added sentence creates a clear and direct conflict within the document as the original text states that all proprietary information should be returned or destroyed, but the changed text permits the Receiving Party to retain one copy. This aligns with the definition of "Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction" where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the Parties relating to the subject matter hereof unless any representati on or warranty made about this Agreement was made fraudulently and, save as may be expressly referred to or referenced herein, supersedes all prior representations, writings, negotiations or understandings with respect hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the Parties relating to the subject matter hereof unless any representati on or warranty made about this Agreement was made fraudulently and, save as may be expressly referred to or referenced herein, supersedes all prior representations, writings, negotiations or understandings with respect hereto. However, any prior agreements relating to intellectual property ownership shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a conflict regarding the scope of the agreement. The original clause establishes the NDA as the *entire* understanding, superseding all prior agreements. The added sentence states that prior agreements about intellectual property *remain* in effect. This contradicts the 'entire understanding' clause, making it unclear which agreements govern intellectual property rights. This undermines the purpose of the integration clause and creates uncertainty about the parties' obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The failure of either party to enforce its rights under this Agreement at any time for any period shall not be construed as a waiver of such rights. If any part, term or provision of this Agr eement is held to be illegal or unenforceable neither the validity, nor enforceability of the remainder of this Agreement shall be affected. Neither Party shall assign or transfer all or any part o f its rights under this Agreement without the consent of th e other Party. This Agreement may not be amended for any other reason without the prior written agreement of both Parties. This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the Parties relating to the subject matter hereof unless any representati on or warranty made about this Agreement was made fraudulently and, save as may be expressly referred to or referenced herein, supersedes all prior representations, writings, negotiations or understandings with respect hereto. However, any prior agreements relating to intellectual property ownership shall remain in full force and effect. 7. This Agreement shall be gover ned by the laws of the jurisdiction in which the Disclosing Party is located (or if the Disclosing Party is based in more than one country, the country in which its headquarters are located) (the "Territory") and the parties agree to submit disputes arisin g out of or in connection with this Agreement to the non -exclusive of the courts in the Territory. [Receiving Party] Signature ___________________________ Name and Tile:_______________________ Address: ____________________________ Date: __________ ____________________
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a sentence stating that prior agreements about intellectual property remain in effect, while the original text (and the contradicted text, which includes the changed text) explicitly states that the agreement supersedes all prior agreements. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency, undermining the 'entire understanding' clause and generating uncertainty about which agreements govern intellectual property rights, therefore causing in-text contradiction.
